sourceMovie.title: The Birdcage
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Birdcage works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Chicago
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Birdcage clicked for you, Chicago (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Birdcage?
faq.answer: Try Chicago, Hairspray, Bring It On—each shares DNA with The Birdcage while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Birdcage?
faq.answer: The Birdcage (1996) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Birdcage worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
